description: Fix recruit mandate: placing bushi after shinto in same fortress province was incorrectly blocked due to overly complex one-per-fortress logic.
steps: 1. Simplify the bushi/monster block in recruitPlaceFigure (src/utils/gameLogic.ts) to use direct province-usage counting instead of comparing usedBaseProvinces.length against basePlacements.
2. New logic: if province hasn't been used for bushi/monster yet, always allow (base slot). If already used, only allow if player is issuer/ally and bonus hasn't been consumed elsewhere.
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
Player with 1 fortress and issuer/ally bonus can place shinto then bushi in same province
One-per-fortress rule still prevents placing 2 bushi in same province when player is NOT issuer/ally
Bonus placement (issuer/ally) allows one repeat province usage for bushi/monster
verification: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in /projects/sandbox/shoguns-ascent and confirm exit code 0
findings: The old logic compared usedBaseProvinces.length to basePlacements (fortress count) to categorize each placement as base vs bonus. When shinto was placed first (decrementing recruitPlacementsRemaining but NOT adding to usedBaseProvinces), the subsequent bushi placement's categorization logic could reach the else branch ('no placements available') in edge cases where the fortress count and bonus calculation interacted poorly. The new logic directly checks how many times a specific province has been used for bushi/monster and only restricts repeated usage.
